input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's your complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS:

---

## 1. Install Xcode (iOS requirement)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- After install, run: `xcode-select --install` (command-line tools)
- Accept the license: `sudo xcodebuild -license accept`
- Open Xcode once to finish component installation
- **Version discovery:** MAUI docs list the minimum Xcode version per .NET release at [learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/supported-platforms](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/supported-platforms)

## 2. Install .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — grab the latest LTS or STS SDK (arm64 for Apple Silicon, x64 for Intel)
- Verify: `dotnet --version`
- **Version discovery:** The MAUI NuGet page ([nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.Maui.Sdk](https://nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.Maui.Sdk)) lists which .NET SDK version each MAUI release targets

## 3. Install the MAUI workload

```bash
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```

- Verify: `dotnet workload list` — should show `maui` (and sub-workloads like `maui-ios`, `maui-android`)
- **Version discovery:** `dotnet workload search maui` shows available versions

## 4. Install Android SDK (via the workload or Android Studio)

**Option A (recommended — automatic):** The `maui-android` workload installs the Android SDK pieces it needs under `~/Library/Developer/Xamarin/android-sdk-macosx/`.

**Option B (manual/full):** Install Android Studio from [developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio), then use SDK Manager to install:
- Android SDK Platform (API level matching MAUI's `TargetFramework`, e.g., `net9.0-android35` → API 35)
- Android SDK Build-Tools
- Android SDK Command-line Tools
- Android Emulator + a system image

Set the env var if using a custom location:
```bash
export ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk"
```

## 5. Install an IDE

| IDE | Source |
|-----|--------|
| **Visual Studio Code** + C# Dev Kit + .NET MAUI extension | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) |
| **Visual Studio for Mac** (discontinued — not recommended for new setups) | — |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) |

VS Code with the **.NET MAUI extension** is now Microsoft's recommended macOS path.

## 6. Install OpenJDK (Android build dependency)

The MAUI android workload bundles a JDK. If you need a standalone one:
- **Source:** [Microsoft OpenJDK](https://learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/download) (v17 recommended)
- Or: `brew install microsoft-openjdk17`
- Verify: `java -version`

## 7. Verification checklist

```bash
# 1. SDK & workloads
dotnet --version
dotnet workload list

# 2. Create and build a test app
dotnet new maui -n MauiTest
cd MauiTest

# 3. Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android   # adjust TFM to your version

# 4. Build for iOS (requires Xcode)
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios

# 5. Run on Android emulator
dotnet build -t:Run -f net9.0-android

# 6. Run on iOS simulator
dotnet build -t:Run -f net9.0-ios
```

## 8. Discovering compatible versions

| What | How |
|------|-----|
| Which .NET SDK for your MAUI version | `dotnet workload search` or [MAUI release notes](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/releases) |
| Which Xcode version | [Supported platforms docs](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/supported-platforms) |
| Which Android API level | Check `TargetFramework` in the template `.csproj` (e.g., `net9.0-android35` → API 35) |
| Which JDK version | [MAUI docs prerequisites](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation) |

---

**TL;DR install order:** Xcode → .NET SDK → `dotnet workload install maui` → VS Code + MAUI extension → create a test project and build for both platforms.

Let me give a thorough but well-organized answer covering all the essentials.
